text: The surgeon reviews the gallbladder anatomy, highlighting the long cystic duct that visibly overhangs the left lobe of the liver.
The surgeon begins the dissection of the gallbladder by separating the posterior leaf, followed by the anterior leaf, using precise surgical techniques.

The surgeon creates a window in Collar's triangle to access the cystic duct, milks the duct, and applies clips before cutting it. A similar window is created for the cystic artery, which is then clipped.
The surgeon dissects the gallbladder away from the liver bed, leaving behind a thin white layer of tissue.
The surgeon extracts the gallbladder specimen, reviewing the gallbladder fossa and the placement of clips. The extracted specimen includes the gallbladder and visible gallstones.
